title: Rustic Brazilian Rye Ceviche
description: A briny, flaky ceviche that uses toasted rye crumbs for a nostalgic Brazilian twist, all FODMAP friendly.
instructions: Dice the snapper into 1/2 inch cubes and place in a shallow bowl.
Add lime juice, sea salt, and black pepper to the fish, and stir gently to coat.
Cover the bowl, refrigerate for 15 minutes to cure the fish.
While the fish cures, place the rye bread on a baking sheet and toast in a 350°F oven until crisp and golden, about 5 minutes.
Transfer toasted rye to a food processor and pulse until coarse crumbs form, then stir in olive oil until the crumbs are lightly coated.
Drain the marinated fish at room temperature for 2 minutes to reduce excess acidity.
Fold the fish gently into the chopped cilantro, bell pepper, and grated carrot.
Toss the fish mixture with the rye crumb coating until evenly distributed.
Transfer to a serving dish and sprinkle with chopped chives.
Serve immediately as a light lunch or snack.
